tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Define the Stage 1 enemy-package baseline
tasks.summary: Define the immutable authoring boundary for the amended five-enemy Stage 1 roster.
tasks.objective: Create package descriptors and deterministic validation without changing accepted combat, movement, encounter or registry contracts.
inputs: Consumed contracts: StableId v1, ContentVersion v1, Combat Messages v1, Encounter Lifecycle v1, Typed Content References v1 and Generated Registry v1.
Accepted roster and boss boundary from AMENDMENT_STAGE1_ENEMIES.md.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning, shared contracts, generated registry outputs, scenes, weapons, movement/combat foundations, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: descriptor inputs only; CS-011 exclusively owns regenerated outputs.
Rollback: remove the four owned files before consumers land. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly the five amended stable IDs and ordinary/elite classifications validate.
Unknown, duplicate, malformed or out-of-bound package behavior fails deterministically.
The elite profile rejects phases, denial pulses, mortars, reinforcements, teleporting and bullet-hell behavior.
verification: Automated: run roster, duplicate-ID, classification and forbidden-capability validator tests.
Manual: contract review confirms all packages fit accepted adapters without a parallel enemy architecture.
tasks.handoff_notes: All enemy packages consume this boundary; generated outputs remain CS-011-owned.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Implement deterministic enemy health, contact, and lifecycle rules
tasks.summary: Provide plain-C# authoritative enemy health, damage, contact and death transitions.
tasks.objective: Create one deterministic enemy actor state used by every package without making Unity objects durable-state owners.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 and Encounter Lifecycle v1; accepted weighted-contact/grace policy and EN-001 descriptors.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: Unity adapters/assets, enemy packages, player state, movement/combat foundations, registries, scenes and persistence.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none; Stage 1 actor state is session-only. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove owned domain files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Damage, bounded contact, death and encounter notifications are deterministic and emitted once.
Enemy state remains engine-independent and cannot own player velocity or durable mission state.
Disposable-impact death and ordinary contact damage are separately representable.
verification: Automated: damage ordering, overkill, repeated contact, grace, disposal and death-once tests.
Manual: architecture review confirms no Unity or MissionRunState ownership leak.
tasks.handoff_notes: EN-003 is the sole Unity bridge consumer for this foundation.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Bridge enemy actors to Unity 2D
tasks.summary: Connect deterministic enemy decisions to targeting, Rigidbody2D movement, hits and contacts.
tasks.objective: Provide bounded Unity 2D adapters shared by enemy packages while preserving plain-C# authority.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1; accepted Unity 2D contact, actor lifecycle and confirmed-hit adapters; EN-002 actor state.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: player adapters, shared movement/combat files, concrete packages, scenes, registries, ProjectSettings and 3D physics.
Serialized ownership: none beyond test-created transient objects. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove four owned files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Adapters apply domain decisions in fixed-step 2D without becoming state authority.
Hits and contacts translate exactly once and disabled/destroyed targets are safe.
No adapter edits player velocity or bypasses accepted contact rules.
verification: Automated: play-mode target loss, contact, hit, disable, restart and 2D-only tests.
Manual: dependency review confirms package folders can consume adapters without editing them.
tasks.handoff_notes: EN-004 through EN-008 may consume but never edit these files.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Pursuer Drone package
tasks.summary: Create the basic walking melee/contact pursuer.
tasks.objective: Implement direct readable pursuit and ordinary contact damage as the baseline enemy role.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 and Encounter Lifecycle v1; accepted movement actor lifecycle and enemy foundation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all other enemy packages, shared adapters, scenes, generated outputs, weapons, player movement and persistence.
Serialized ownership: every asset under PursuerDrone only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes enemy.pursuer-drone input only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary package-owned presentation expires at final-art replacement.
acceptance_criteria: The drone approaches the player directly and deals bounded ordinary contact damage.
Pursuit stops safely on death, target loss or session reset.
Shape, motion and warning remain readable without color.
verification: Automated: pursuit, contact cadence, target-loss, death and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm it reads as the uncomplicated durable-enough baseline.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with EN-005 through EN-008; never edit their folders.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Ram Droid package
tasks.summary: Create the small fast low-health disposable impact attacker.
tasks.objective: Implement readable collision pressure with one bounded impact hit followed by self-destruction.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1; accepted weighted contact/grace and Unity 2D contact adapters; enemy foundation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: other packages, shared adapters, player velocity, scenes, registries and persistence.
Serialized ownership: every asset under RamDroid only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes enemy.ram-droid input only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary presentation expires at final-art replacement.
acceptance_criteria: The droid is faster, smaller and lower-health than the Pursuer Drone.
First valid player impact deals one bounded hit and destroys the droid exactly once.
Grace, wall contact and non-player collision cannot duplicate impact damage.
verification: Automated: speed/health relation, impact-once, grace, wall, death and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm a clear non-color warning and fair reaction window.
tasks.handoff_notes: Original Ram Droid identity; disposable collision pressure only.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Mobile Blaster Droid package
tasks.summary: Create a moving ranged enemy that reuses the accepted Blaster projectile.
tasks.objective: Implement simple movement, readable firing timing and bounded ranged pressure without cloning weapon logic.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1; accepted Unity 2D execution adapter, projectile primitive, Blaster package and enemy foundation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: weapon package, projectile primitive, other enemies, shared adapters, scenes, registries and persistence.
Serialized ownership: every asset under MobileBlasterDroid only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes enemy.mobile-blaster-droid input only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary presentation expires at final-art replacement.
acceptance_criteria: The droid moves using simple deterministic positioning and fires the accepted Blaster projectile profile.
Wind-up, projectile direction and recovery are readable and bounded.
Target loss, death and restart cancel pending fire safely.
verification: Automated: movement, cadence, projectile reuse, target-loss, death and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm ranged pressure remains distinguishable from the turret.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with other package tasks and must not edit WP-owned files.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Blaster Turret package
tasks.summary: Create a stationary ranged enemy using the accepted Blaster projectile.
tasks.objective: Implement a fixed-position shooter with a clear line-of-fire warning and simple cadence.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1; accepted projectile primitive, Blaster package, Unity 2D execution adapter and enemy foundation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: weapon/projectile implementation, other enemies, shared adapters, scenes, registries and persistence.
Serialized ownership: every asset under BlasterTurret only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes enemy.blaster-turret input only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary presentation expires at final-art replacement.
acceptance_criteria: The turret remains stationary and fires the accepted Blaster projectile with deterministic timing.
Line-of-fire warning remains legible without color and precedes every shot.
Obstruction, target loss, death and restart cannot leave stale shots.
verification: Automated: stationary, cadence, obstruction, target-loss, death and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm warning clarity and identity versus the mobile shooter.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with other package tasks and owns no shared projectile asset.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the easy Four-Blaster Elite package
tasks.summary: Create the approachable first boss with four Blaster origins and mild telegraphed spread.
tasks.objective: Implement one simple deterministic boss cadence with generous recovery and none of the superseded Foreman complexity.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Encounter Lifecycle v1 and Combat Messages v1; accepted behavior pipeline, projectile/execution adapters, Blaster package and enemy foundation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: player four-mount state, weapon package, other enemies, shared adapters, scenes, reward authority, Prototype Overseer and persistence.
Serialized ownership: every asset under FourBlasterElite only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes enemy.four-blaster-elite input only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary boss presentation expires at final-art replacement.
acceptance_criteria: One health model drives four Blaster origins with mild bounded telegraphed spread and deterministic safe recovery.
The boss has no phase, denial pulse, mortar, reinforcement, teleport, complex repositioning or bullet-hell barrage.
Completion emits once and all warnings remain color-independent and reduced-effects readable.
verification: Automated: four-origin ordering, spread cap, cadence, recovery, completion-once, death and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm the first boss is easy, fair and clearly not the Prototype Overseer.
tasks.handoff_notes: Keep this intentionally simpler than Prototype Overseer and independent of player four-mount authority.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Validate enemy packages and registry inputs
tasks.summary: Validate the five packages together without editing generated registry outputs.
tasks.objective: Prove stable identities, package isolation, roster completeness and registry-input determinism.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Typed Content References v1 and Generated Registry v1; all five package descriptors.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: CS-011 generated outputs/generator, package folders, scenes, adapters, tuning and persistence.
Serialized ownership: fixture JSON only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: validates inputs only; outputs must be regenerated by CS-011 owner.
Rollback: remove three owned files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly four ordinary IDs and one elite ID validate with no duplicate ownership.
Registry input is deterministic and drift is reported without manual output conflict resolution.
Every package declares its package-owned serialized assets and temporary-presentation debt.
verification: Automated: package matrix, stable ordering, duplicate, missing-role and drift tests.
Manual: ownership audit confirms no exact or parent-folder overlap.
tasks.handoff_notes: EN-010 and EN-011 consume the validated roster and may proceed in parallel.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Compose benchmark-arena enemy encounters
tasks.summary: Define resettable controlled encounters for each role, mixed groups and the first boss.
tasks.objective: Add package-owned encounter fixtures loaded by the evidence arena without editing its scene.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Encounter Lifecycle v1; evidence arena/reset harness, fixed weapon loadouts and validated enemy roster.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: EH-004 benchmark scene, enemy/weapon packages, shared encounter contracts, registries and persistence.
Serialized ownership: assets under Stage1Benchmark only; Stage1BenchmarkArena.unity is read-only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: encounter inputs only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: Stage 1 benchmark fixtures expire after the formal gate.
acceptance_criteria: Fixtures cover each enemy alone, representative mixed pressure and a standalone Four-Blaster Elite fight.
Every scenario resets deterministically and respects bounded counts/performance budgets.
No fixture claims pacing or quality acceptance before EN-013.
verification: Automated: spawn order, reset, completion-once, count budget and missing-ID tests.
Manual/playable: rapidly select and replay every fixture in the unmodified arena.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with EN-011 after EN-009.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Compose short-route encounters and bounded hazards
tasks.summary: Define room-to-room enemy pressure, simple hazards and the elite endpoint for the short route.
tasks.objective: Project deterministic encounter data into the route shell without letting scenes own durable state.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Room Projection v1 and Encounter Lifecycle v1; short-route/reset shells, fixed loadouts and validated enemy roster.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: EH-005 route scene, enemy/weapon packages, shared contracts, registries, MissionRunState and persistence.
Serialized ownership: assets under Stage1ShortRoute only; Stage1ShortRouteShell.unity is read-only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: encounter/hazard inputs only.
Rollback: remove package folder/test. Prototype debt: temporary route composition expires when Stage 2 factory rooms replace it.
acceptance_criteria: The route projects deterministic ordinary encounters, bounded non-color hazards and one Four-Blaster Elite endpoint.
Loaded rooms remain projections, retreat rules are respected and elite lockdown releases on completion.
Rapid restart restores the frozen route without stale enemies, hazards or projectiles.
verification: Automated: projection, room order, retreat, lockdown, hazard, restart and completion-once tests.
Manual/playable: complete the unmodified route shell with fixed loadouts and inspect warning readability.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with EN-010; EH-005 retains exclusive scene ownership.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Add Stage 1 enemy and route evidence scenarios
tasks.summary: Produce immutable evidence for enemy behavior, arena encounters and short-route completion.
tasks.objective: Verify determinism, readability prerequisites, diagnostics, restart and performance without claiming human quality acceptance.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1, Diagnostics and Run Validity v1, Room Projection v1 and Encounter Lifecycle v1; prior movement/combat/weapon evidence and both encounter sets.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: evidence-owned scenes/framework, packages, encounter definitions, generated registries, tuning and persistence.
Serialized ownership: stage1-enemy-route-evidence-v1.json only; both scenes are read-only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove three owned files. Prototype debt: Stage 1 evidence fixture expires after the formal gate.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence covers all five enemies, mixed arena encounters, short-route completion and the approachable boss boundary.
Restart, identity, diagnostics, manifests and invalid-session classification are reproducible.
Representative density remains inside recorded Stage 1 performance budgets and missing warnings invalidate evidence.
verification: Automated: run edit/play/Windows smoke entrypoints for behavior, route, restart, diagnostics, checksum and performance scenarios.
Manual/playable: prepare the immutable EN-013 review package without asserting feel, pacing or readability acceptance.
tasks.handoff_notes: EN-013 is the explicit human quality gate for this batch.

tasks.context: S1.3 frozen question: can four deliberately simple ordinary enemies, an approachable Four-Blaster Elite and a short factory route produce readable pressure and voluntary replay within the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Record the human enemy, boss, and route quality gate
tasks.summary: Record the human playable review required before downstream work treats S1.3 quality as accepted.
tasks.objective: Evaluate movement and aiming interaction, enemy identity, audiovisual feedback, encounter pacing, boss fairness, route readability and UI warning quality.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics and Run Validity v1; accepted weapon human gate and immutable EN-012 evidence package.
outputs: Machine-readable pass/fail checklist and bounded findings
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all implementation, tuning, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, registries, evidence framework and persistence.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove review artifacts and return gate to blocked. Prototype debt: records package presentation/route-fixture dispositions but cannot waive blocking debt.
Any required fix becomes a separately reviewed bounded follow-up; this task records evidence only.
acceptance_criteria: Human review records pass/fail for movement feel, aiming, every enemy identity, audiovisual cues, pacing, warnings, reduced effects, route quality and first-boss fairness.
The Four-Blaster Elite is approachable, readable and free of superseded complex behaviors.
Every blocking finding identifies evidence and a bounded follow-up; failures are not silently accepted.
verification: Automated: validate checklist shape, required dimensions, fingerprints and EN-012 links.
Manual/playable: complete frozen arena and route cases with fixed loadouts and reduced-effects settings.
Review: verify human identity, date, fingerprints and evidence links.
tasks.handoff_notes: Downstream Stage 1 quality claims must consume this recorded gate.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.40 focused lead day. Batch direct spend totals 5.75 focused lead days, preserving 4.25 days of the S1.3 cap for human review reserve and bounded evidence-led follow-up.
